name: ProgressIndicator
description: Progress indicators show that the app is working — loading a report, exporting trips, fetching a page of vehicles. They reassure the operator that something is happening and, when possible, how far along it is.
keyRule: Use determinate progress when you know the percentage; indeterminate when you don't. For content that's loading into a known layout (tables, cards), prefer skeletons over spinners.

variants.name: Linear determinate
variants.whenToUse: A known-percentage task with a layout to sit above/below (uploads, exports).

variants.name: Linear indeterminate
variants.whenToUse: Loading where duration is unknown; spans a region's width.

anatomy.name: Track
anatomy.description: the full extent (linear) or the unfilled ring.

behaviors.name: Determinate vs indeterminate
behaviors.description: Switch to determinate as soon as a real percentage is available; don't fake progress.

behaviors.name: Delay
behaviors.description: For fast operations, delay showing a spinner briefly to avoid a flicker (the portal has delayed-spinner variants for this).

behaviors.name: Skeletons
behaviors.description: When loading into a known structure, skeleton placeholders feel faster and less jarring than a spinner.

states.treatment: Hide, or show 100% briefly before dismissing.

props.name: type
props.description: Linear spans a region's width; circular is a compact/inline indicator.

props.name: variant
props.description: Determinate shows a known percentage; indeterminate animates for unknown duration.

props.name: size
props.description: Circular size in px. Ignored for linear.

props.name: maxWidth
props.description: Max width for the linear track — px number (default 300) or any CSS length, e.g. '100%' to span a host region (as DataTable's loading bar does). Ignored for circular.

doThis: Use determinate progress when you know the percentage.
dontDoThis: Don't block the whole screen for a small async action.
Don't flash a spinner for sub-second waits.
accessibility: Use progressbar role with aria-valuenow (determinate) or busy state (indeterminate).
Give it an accessible label describing what's loading.

related.name: Snackbar
related.why: Announce completion when the wait ends

related.name: Slider
related.why: Looks similar but is input, not status — don't confuse them
